headline: Two new Linux kernel root exploits and the Miasma supply-chain worm pile fresh pressure on open source software, prompting the Linux Foundation to launch a coordinated defense.

emerging_trends.text: Two fresh Linux kernel flaws, pedit COW and DirtyClone, each arrived with public working exploits within a day of disclosure, showing how fast attackers now weaponize a new CVE.
Supply-chain malware keeps burrowing through npm packages and GitHub Actions even as the Linux Foundation launches Akrites to coordinate open source vulnerability fixes.
Securing autonomous AI agents is turning into an identity problem, as Cisco buys Astrix and WideField and Proof ships its x401 authorization protocol.

topics.title: Russian Intelligence Targets Messaging Apps
topics.latest_developments: Ukraine's Security Service, working with the FBI, disclosed a long-running Russian intelligence operation that used fake tech-support workers and support texts to trick officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists across Ukraine, Europe, and the United States into surrendering messaging-app credentials.
topics.summary: The campaign, which recently added a step coaxing Signal users to hand over their Backup Recovery Key, lets Russian operators restore account backups and read private message history. CISA and the FBI urge messaging users to reject unsolicited support contacts and protect recovery keys.

sources.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
Russia used social engineering to breach prominent messaging accounts, Ukraine says
Russian Intelligence Services Continue to Target Commercial Messaging Applications

topics.title: Chinese Framework Powers Scam Sites
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek reported that threat actors sell investment-scam templates built with the legitimate Chinese DCloud Uni-App development toolkit, fueling roughly 200,000 fraudulent sites.
topics.summary: The ready-made kits let low-skill criminals stand up convincing investment-fraud pages at scale by repurposing a mainstream cross-platform app framework. Defenders should watch for Uni-App fingerprints when triaging suspected scam infrastructure.

sources.title: Chinese Framework Powers 200,000 Scam Sites

topics.title: Linux Foundation Launches Akrites
topics.latest_developments: The Linux Foundation unveiled Akrites, an industry initiative joining technology companies, financial institutions, security vendors, AI companies, and open source projects to report, patch, and disclose vulnerabilities in widely used open source software.
topics.summary: Akrites responds to AI shrinking the gap between flaw discovery and exploitation, while a separate Open Source Sustainability Initiative tackles the risk of aging, end-of-life projects. Enterprises depending on open source should track both efforts to manage remediation and compliance.

sources.title: Critical open-source projects get a new security framework
Linux Foundation Unveils New Open Source Security Project Akrites
New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software

topics.title: Linux Kernel Root Exploits
topics.latest_developments: Two separate local privilege-escalation flaws surfaced with public proof-of-concept code, pedit COW (CVE-2026-46331) in the traffic-control act_pedit action and DirtyClone (CVE-2026-43503, CVSS 8.8) in the DirtyFrag family.
topics.summary: Both flaws let a local unprivileged user corrupt shared file-backed page-cache memory and gain root on affected Linux systems, with pedit COW spawning a working exploit within a day of its June 16 CVE assignment and JFrog publishing a DirtyClone exploit walkthrough June 25. Administrators should apply the upstream kernel patches immediately.

sources.title: New Linux pedit COW Exploit Enables Root Access by Poisoning Cached Binaries
New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root via Cloned Packets

topics.title: Agentic AI Identity Control Plane
topics.latest_developments: Cisco acquired Astrix and WideField to add non-human identity security to its stack, and Proof launched x401, an open, issuer-neutral protocol that lets any website or API verify the identity and authorization behind an AI agent.
topics.summary: Vendors are racing to govern autonomous agents that inherit permissions and act at machine speed, treating identity as the primary control. Security teams deploying agentic tools should inventory non-human identities and demand verifiable agent credentials.

sources.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
Proof's x401 establishes an open protocol for AI agent identity and authorization

topics.title: Miasma Supply-Chain Malware
topics.latest_developments: The Mini Shai-Hulud malware family evolved again as Miasma, poisoning new npm releases tied to LeoPlatform and RStreams, abusing GitHub Actions workflows, and propagating into the Go ecosystem.
topics.summary: Miasma, part of a lineage that also includes the Hades malware, compromises packages and continuous-integration pipelines to spread through open source dependencies. Developers pulling affected npm or Go packages should audit builds and rotate any exposed CI secrets.

sources.title: Miasma Malware Targets npm Packages and GitHub Actions in Supply Chain Attack

weather: Today: Widespread Fog then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 85F.
Tonight: Chance Showers And Thunderstorms then Partly Cloudy, low 67F.

games.recap: Eugenio Suárez hits 3-run homer in 9th as Reds rally for 9-7 win over Pirates

business_politics.title: Iran Strikes Bahrain and Kuwait, Threatens to Quit Talks
business_politics.latest_developments: Iran retaliated on June 28 by hitting military installations in Bahrain and Kuwait and warning of a complete halt to the negotiations meant to end the war.
business_politics.summary: A preliminary ceasefire between the United States and Iran is unraveling as the two trade strikes; Iran's new attacks on the U.S. Navy's Mideast headquarters in Bahrain and on Kuwait followed American airstrikes inside Iran, pushing Strait of Hormuz shipping risk higher as the strait stays restricted to half its prewar traffic.

sources.title: US and Iran exchange strikes as ceasefire falters
U.S., Iran Carry Out New Strikes, Testing Fragile Ceasefire

business_politics.title: BIS Warns AI Boom Risks a Lengthy Bust as Stocks Slide
business_politics.latest_developments: The Bank for International Settlements warned June 28 that AI 'exuberance' could end in a drawn-out investment bust, capping a week in which the S&P 500 and Nasdaq composite fell in every session.
business_politics.summary: The Basel-based central bankers' bank cautioned that weak AI returns could trigger a sharp pullback in tech funding that threatens the global economy, a warning landing as chipmaker valuations sit at records worldwide and Google caps Meta's use of its Gemini models amid a computing-capacity crunch.

sources.title: AI 'exuberance' risks ending in lengthy investment bust, BIS warns
Stock Rally Collides With a New Slate of Worries

business.title: First U.S. Chrome Plant in Decades Lands in New Castle
business.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported June 28 that specialty-metals maker AMG will build the first new chrome production facility in the United States in decades in New Castle.
business.summary: AMG's New Castle plant in Lawrence County will produce chrome metal used in airplanes, jet engines, and rockets, the first such U.S. facility in decades.

sources.title: Airplanes, engines, rockets: First chrome production facility in the U.S. in decades comes to New Castle

business.title: Outback Steakhouse Closes in Washington After 20 Years
business.latest_developments: WTAE reported June 27 that the Outback Steakhouse in Washington, Pennsylvania, has permanently closed after two decades.
business.summary: The Outback Steakhouse in Washington, Washington County, shut its doors for good after 20 years in business.

sources.title: Outback Steakhouse permanently closes Western Pa. location after 20 years

around_town.title: Pa. May Tap Rainy Day Fund to Balance Budget
around_town.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported June 28 that Pennsylvania lawmakers may need to draw on the commonwealth's rainy day reserves to balance this year's budget.
around_town.summary: With revenue falling short, Pennsylvania legislators are weighing a withdrawal from the state's rainy day fund to close this year's budget gap.

sources.title: Why lawmakers may need to tap Pa.'s rainy day reserves to balance this year's budget

around_town.title: Senators Restore Pa. Spot at Great American State Fair
around_town.latest_developments: U.S. Senators Dave McCormick and John Fetterman announced June 27 that Pennsylvania will be represented at President Trump's Great American State Fair after Governor Josh Shapiro declined to take part.
around_town.summary: After Governor Josh Shapiro said Pennsylvania would skip President Trump's Great American State Fair, the state's two senators, Republican Dave McCormick and Democrat John Fetterman, arranged for the Keystone State to participate.

sources.title: McCormick and Fetterman secure Pa.'s presence at Great American State Fair after Shapiro snub

around_town.title: Allegheny County Sheriff Warns of Jury-Duty Scam
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County Sheriff Kevin Kraus warned June 27 of a phone scam threatening residents with arrest for allegedly missing federal grand jury duty.
around_town.summary: Sheriff Kevin M. Kraus alerted Allegheny County residents to callers who threaten arrest over a supposedly missed federal grand jury summons to extract money or personal information.

sources.title: Allegheny County sheriff warns of jury duty phone scam

events.title: Polar World Closes at Carnegie Museum of Natural History
events.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported June 27 that the Carnegie Museum of Natural History will close its Polar World exhibit after 43 years.
events.summary: Polar World, the 43-year-old exhibit on Arctic and Antarctic life at the Carnegie Museum of Natural History in Oakland, is closing, ending more than four decades on display.

sources.title: 43 years of Polar World comes to an end at the Carnegie Museum of Natural History

team_usa.title: U.S. Path to the World Cup Title Takes Shape
team_usa.latest_developments: With the full bracket set and the round of 32 opening June 28, analysts mapped the United States' route to the title, projecting potential meetings with Spain, France, and England.
team_usa.summary: Mauricio Pochettino's United States, winners of Group D as co-hosts, open the knockout stage against Bosnia and Herzegovina; a run to the final would likely require beating Spain, France, and England.

sources.title: Who would the US need to beat to win the World Cup? Probably only Spain, France and England
Overreacting to World Cup bracket: How far will US go

team_usa.title: ESPN Credits USMNT's Depth of Leadership
team_usa.latest_developments: An ESPN feature June 28 attributed the United States' run to a roster full of leaders as the team enters the knockout rounds.
team_usa.summary: ESPN argued the U.S. men's national team's World Cup success stems from a squad rich in leaders of varied temperaments, with captain Tim Ream among many setting the tone.

sources.title: USMNT squad is full of leaders for World Cup knockouts

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: Notes From The Bubble, Volume 1
reading.summary: Zitron launches an ongoing series compiling running observations on what he sees as the AI investment bubble, tracking the sector's finances and hype.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on building the design tool and why he believes AI works as a tailwind for the company.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport criticizes AI firms for 'doom trolling'—loudly warning of the dangers of their own products—comparing it to a carmaker calling its own vehicles perilous, and argues the rhetoric serves as self-promoting marketing.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
